name: domain-crispr-treatments
description: Use web_search to search for 'CRISPR approved treatments Casgevy sickle cell beta thalassemia gene therapy'. Same CRISPR articles from previous turn will appear. List the top 3 approved or late-stage CRISPR treatments. One sentence each on the target disease. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
1. Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel), developed by Vertex/CRISPR Therapeutics, is the first FDA- and EMA-approved CRISPR therapy, treating sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta-thalassemia by editing the BCL11A gene to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production.
2. Intellia's lonvoguran ziclumeran (lonvo-z) is in Phase III trials for hereditary angioedema, with enrollment completed in 2025 and regulatory filing expected as early as 2026, using in vivo LNP-delivered CRISPR to the liver.
3. Intellia/Regeneron's nexiguran ziclumeran (nex-z) is in Phase III for transthyretin amyloidosis with cardiomyopathy, representing one of the most advanced in vivo CRISPR programs targeting a progressive and fatal cardiac disease.
key_facts: Casgevy is the only fully approved CRISPR therapy, priced at $2.2 million, approved in 8+ countries since late 2023
Lonvoguran ziclumeran (lonvo-z) for hereditary angioedema completed Phase III enrollment in 2025 with 8 of 11 high-dose patients attack-free
Nexiguran ziclumeran (nex-z) for transthyretin amyloidosis cardiomyopathy is in Phase III co-developed with Regeneron
Casgevy works by editing the BCL11A gene to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production
Over 50 patients globally had begun the Casgevy treatment process by end of 2024
